fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: Arkansas CNA Certification Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to Arkansas - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity arkansas, how to transfer CNA license to arkansas, arkansas CNA endorsement application

board.name: Arkansas State Board of Nursing
board.mailing_address:
Arkansas Department of Human Services
Office of Long Term Care
P.O. Box 8100
Little Rock, AR 72203-8100

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in Arkansas.

steps.title: Complete Approved Training Program
steps.description: Enroll in and successfully complete a Nurse Aide Training and Competency Evaluation Program (NATCEP) approved by the Arkansas Department of Human Services (DHS) Office of Long Term Care.

steps.title: Register for Examination
steps.description: After completing your training, register for the National Nurse Aide Assessment Program (NNAAP) examination through Credentia, the state's official testing vendor.

steps.title: Pass NNAAP Examination
steps.description: Successfully pass both the written/oral and the skills competency portions of the NNAAP examination administered by Credentia.

steps.title: Registry Listing & Background Check
steps.description: Upon successful completion of the NNAAP exam, the testing vendor (Credentia) will notify the Arkansas Nurse Aide Registry, and your name will be added to the official registry after any necessary background checks. No separate application is typically submitted directly to the Registry for initial listing by exam.

steps.title: Verify Registry Status
steps.description: Once listed, you can verify your active status on the Arkansas Nurse Aide Registry via the online 'Search the Nurse Aide Registry' tool, accessible from the Arkansas Nurse Aide Registry (DHS OLTC) page.

documents.detail: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: How much does Arkansas CNA endorsement cost?
faqs.answer: The endorsement application fee is $25. Additional costs may include background check fees.

faqs.question: Is a background check required?
faqs.answer: Yes, a fingerprint-based background check is required in Arkansas.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my CNA certification to Arkansas?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Arkansas while waiting?
faqs.answer: Check with the state board about temporary practice permits while your endorsement is being processed.

reciprocity.notes: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become a CNA in AR.

hero.title: Become a CNA in Arkansas
hero.dek: To become a Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA) in Arkansas, individuals must complete a state-approved training program and pass the NNAAP examination, which results in placement on the Arkansas Nurse Aide Registry managed by the DHS Office of Long Term Care.

seo.title: How to Become a CNA in Arkansas

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the relevant section (e.g., 'New Nurse Aides', 'Endorsement') on the Arkansas Nurse Aide Registry page to find information, download application forms, or access links related to training and examination. For initial certification by exam, there isn't an online application portal; the process is managed through training programs and the testing vendor.
